source: NvChad - Using vale in NvChad: the .vale.ini file, vale sync, styles, and wiring vale into lint.lua

options: In the root of your home folder (~/)
questions.why: The .vale.ini goes in your home root.
vale sync downloads the styles defined in .vale.ini.

questions.q: From the vale binary's directory, what command generates the styles folder?
questions.why: Run ./vale sync from the mason packages/vale directory.

questions.q: Which base and supplementary styles does the guide choose?
options: Red Hat Documentation Style Guide, plus alex
questions.why: Red Hat as base, alex as the optional supplementary style.
alex flags insensitive or exclusionary language.

questions.q: To hide the styles folder, which .vale.ini option do you change?
questions.why: Set StylesPath to a hidden name like .vale_styles.

questions.q: The final step that actually enables vale for markdown is...
options: adding "vale" to the markdown linters in lua/configs/lint.lua
questions.why: Add vale to markdown = { ... } in configs/lint.lua.

questions.q: When does vale check a newly created (not yet saved) file?
options: When you leave INSERT mode (on write / BufWritePost), not while inserting
questions.why: Linting runs on BufWritePost, keeping insert mode uncluttered.
